meta.dataset: On The Door — live notice board
meta.counter_notes: Reviews this month (Jul 2026): El Rincón pending (Pier One was decided 30 Jun, i.e. last month). Closures this quarter (Q3 2026, from 1 Jul): none recorded — Corsica Studios closed 29 Mar 2026 (Q1).

notices.headline: Westminster consults on 2026 licensing policy — new Special Consideration Zones around the West End
notices.detail: Draft SoLP keeps the West End CIZ (trimmed slightly NE of Covent Garden), removes the Edgware Road and Queensway/Bayswater CIZs, rings the West End with large new Special Consideration Zones, and reworks Core Hours by premises type. Consultation closes 5pm, 9 August 2026.

notices.headline: NTIA brands Westminster's CIA consultation potentially unlawful
notices.detail: NTIA alleges the consultation (part of the After Dark strategy) was not fair, transparent or accessible, and that much of the licensed trade didn't know it was happening.

notices.headline: El Rincón review: public representations close 16 July — hearing date still to be listed
notices.detail: Avon & Somerset Police requested Bristol City Council review the North Street bar's premises licence, citing crime and disorder, public nuisance and public safety (application valid 18 Jun 2026, ref 26/03363/PRREV). Representations close 16 July 2026; the hearing must then be listed within 20 working days.

notices.headline: High Court rejects appeal over Pier One Club revocation
notices.detail: Judgment 30 June 2026. Licence revoked Nov 2023 after Newham Council and Met Police raised women's safety concerns incl. two alleged rapes. Landlord's route back requires variation + new operator under stringent conditions.

notices.headline: Corsica Studios closes after 25 years — S106 refurb, handback in 2027
notices.detail: Closed 29 March 2026 amid the Elephant & Castle development. Venue to be soundproofed and refurbished at developer Delancey's cost under a Section 106 agreement, with long-term lease and noise protections, returning 2027.

notices.headline: Brighton scraps its CIZ — 'City Safety Area' takes over, presumption of refusal dropped
notices.detail: New SoLP adopted 18 Dec 2025, effective 5 Jan 2026. Same boundary as the old CIZ but no blanket refusal presumption; matrix-based scrutiny plus a new 'good operator' policy. Special Stress Area retained.

notices.headline: Leeds approves CIA 2026–28 after back-to-back expansions
notices.detail: Approved by Licensing Committee Jan 2026, following the Beeston CIA addition (Jan 2025) and the Headingley Lane/Hyde Park expansion (2025). Full 232-page assessment only available by email request.

notices.headline: Southwark's 2026–31 policy lands with CIAs intact and a wider late-night levy on the table
notices.detail: Borough & Bankside and Camberwell CIAs retained; proposal to extend the Late Night Levy to late-night refreshment premises trading midnight–6am (114 premises, ~£127k revenue).

notices.headline: Confirmed: Hackney's Shoreditch CIA has been in force since April 2025 — and the council's policy page still doesn't say so
notices.detail: Full Council decided 26 Feb 2025 to publish a Cumulative Impact Assessment for Shoreditch and designate Dalston a Special Consideration Area (not a CIA), effective 1 Apr 2025. As of 13 Jul 2026 the council's licensing policy page still shows only the 2023 SoLP with no mention of either designation.

notices.headline: Edinburgh ends overprovision — Old Town, Princes Street, Tollcross, Leith Street opened up
notices.detail: Board voted 5–3 on 23 June 2025 to scrap the overprovision policy despite evidence from Police Scotland, NHS Lothian and Alcohol Focus Scotland.

notices.headline: Camden drops Camden Town and Seven Dials cumulative impact areas
notices.detail: SoLP 2025–2030: committee found insufficient evidence to sustain a CIA. Resident associations objected to the removal.

items: One UK nightclub closes roughly every two days
26% of UK towns/cities that had a nightclub in 2020 now have none
16% of towns have lost all late-night venues entirely
~400 club closures since 2020 (over a third of the 2020 stock)
